does: This paper articulates a framework for understanding proactive coding agents, distinguishing proactivity from autonomy by focusing on an agent's ability to independently identify relevant changes and opportunities in the development lifecycle before explicit user requests. The authors propose that proactive agents should be evaluated through their insight policy—the mechanism determining what matters next, what evidence supports decisions, whether to surface information, and how to adapt from feedback. The core contribution is establishing acceptance criteria and evaluation metrics for proactive long-horizon tasks, grounded in principles of mixed-initiative interaction, moving beyond simple activity measures to assess whether unsolicited agent behavior actually provides useful value.
connects: This work builds on the growing literature of autonomous coding agents that have evolved from simple inline code completion to systems managing repositories, pull requests, and issue responses. It engages with broader research on autonomous systems in software engineering and extends prior discussions of agent autonomy by critically examining what proactivity adds beyond mere independence of action. The paper connects to foundational work on mixed-initiative human-AI collaboration, which emphasizes shared decision-making and appropriate timing of agent contributions, and relates to research on information filtering and interruption management in human-computer interaction, translating these concepts into the software development domain.
enables: This framework enables future work on designing and evaluating coding agents that meaningfully support developers rather than simply automating tasks. By establishing insight policy as a central evaluation criterion, it opens research directions into how agents should learn developer preferences, recognize contextually important changes across disparate tools and signals, and optimize for interruption timing and communication clarity. The work points toward development of better metrics for proactive behavior in software engineering, systems that can maintain and adapt long-term developer models across sessions, and hybrid approaches where agent initiative is genuinely calibrated to developer needs rather than defaulting to maximum autonomy.
